title: Design reviewed worktree cleanup apply path
goal: Define and implement a guarded cleanup path for worktree-hygiene candidates without deleting worktrees from a report alone.

metadata.source: Registered after PR #253 intentionally shipped worktree-hygiene as read-only only.
metadata.safety_boundary: Cleanup must be reviewed, path-scoped, branch-aware, dirty-state-protected, process-aware and reversible where possible. The external worktree-admin gate and current exact-path lease check remain mandatory operator preconditions.

acceptance.assertion: Apply mode accepts only explicit absolute candidate paths from a reviewed plan, not all hygiene findings.
Review is bound to repository identity and exact candidate path, head, branch and safety-state hashes; stale or modified plans fail closed before an effect.
Canonical, dirty, missing, locked, procfs-observably process-used or not-fully-merged worktrees are refused; no force mode exists.
Cleanup calls git worktree remove without force and never deletes local or remote branches.
If a later candidate or post-clean gate fails, already removed worktrees are restored where Git still permits exact reconstruction from the reviewed branch or detached head.
After cleanup, worktree-hygiene emits a fresh post-clean report proving the reviewed paths are absent while unrelated worktrees remain.
Documentation requires the short Bureau worktree-admin gate and a separate current check for foreign exact path leases; the hygiene report never claims authority over Grabowski lease truth.
